sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, presents with a one-week history of neck pain following a car accident.
He describes the pain as a 7/10, which worsens with movement of the neck.
The pain is localized to the back of the neck and has not been constant.
He has tried ibuprofen, which provided some relief, but the pain remains at a 5/10.
The patient reports having had headaches early on, but they have subsided.
He denies any visual disturbances or hearing problems.
He has a history of playing football years ago, but no previous neck issues.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms in other organs that are relevant to the initial presentation.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibited positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, pain with flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending.
The patient also had positive pain with rotation of the neck.
Test Results: The patient had an x-ray, which showed no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has neck strain.
Problem list: Neck strain; unknown status for other conditions.
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for anti-inflammatory purposes.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to be conservative with neck movement and to try to relax the neck.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any nerve impingement or other conditions.
I referred the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient once the MRI results are available to discuss further management.
